titleStmt.title: 254. Memorandum From the Presidentʼs Assistant for National Security Affairs (Kissinger) to President ) to President Nixon

div.head: 254. Memorandum From the Presidentʼs Assistant for National Security Affairs (Kissinger) to President Nixon Source: National Archives, Nixon Presidential Materials, NSC Files, Box 593, Country Files, Middle East—Greece, Vol. I Jan 69–Oct 70. Confidential. Sent for information. Drafted by Saunders on September 23. A notation on the memorandum indicates it was returned on October 6. In a note attached to Saundersʼs copy of this memorandum, Haig commented: “HAK—This looks like more dynamite. I suspect we should thank this fellow and tell him to let it drop.” Kissinger minuted: “I agree. HK” (Ibid., Saunders Subject Files, Box 1234, Greece 6/1/69–9/30/69)
div.p: Tom Evans in the attached memo has passed on to you a message from King Constantine.Not printed. Evansʼs discussion with the King took place at a dinner party in Copenhagen, Denmark during the last week of July. The King requests that the newly appointed Ambassador to GreeceHenry J. Tasca. The Senate confirmed his appointment on December 20, 1969. stop off for a chat with the King on his way through Rome. Constantine argues that this would not upset the Military Government in Athens because the Ambassador will be presenting his credentials to a Regent who is the Kingʼs representative in Athens. (Tab A)
I will take no action unless you disagree.
